task_updates.executor_notes: Refactored `PaymentService.request_payment(...)` into a thin orchestrator that now delegates to exactly five helpers: `_normalize_inputs`, `_detect_collision_and_idempotent_row`, `_derive_amounts`, `_enforce_caps`, and `_persist_row`. The public signature stayed unchanged, the existing log messages were preserved as code motion rather than rewritten behavior, and the refactor held under both the targeted payment-module run and the full pytest suite without needing refactor-specific test edits.
commands_run: pytest tests/test_payment_reconcile.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_social_route_tools.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --tb=no -q --no-header

task_updates.executor_notes: The investigation separated the DB claim semantics from the real startup overlap. `claim_due_payment_requests` remains structurally safe at the `queued -> processing` boundary under `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, but the worker loop could begin before `recover_inflight()` finished and re-scan fresh `processing/submitted` rows during startup. I added the specified 150-second reclaim guard in `PaymentService.recover_inflight()` and a logical-ordering sanity-check test in `tests/test_payment_race.py`; the new test and the full suite both pass.
commands_run: pytest tests/test_payment_reconcile.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_social_route_tools.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --tb=no -q --no-header

task_updates.executor_notes: Reproduced the same-recipient admin collision at the downstream flow using two distinct `source_channel_id` values so the intent-dedupe layer was bypassed on purpose. The fix is the required millisecond-precision `producer_ref` in `execute_initiate_payment(...)`, and the regression test drives both intents through `_start_admin_payment_flow` to verify they persist as separate payments. The targeted payment/admin modules and the full suite pass with the fix in place.
commands_run: pytest tests/test_payment_reconcile.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_social_route_tools.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --tb=no -q --no-header

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `hypothesis` to `requirements.txt` and shipped `tests/test_payment_state_machine.py` with a `RuleBasedStateMachine` that exercises `create_payment`, `confirm_payment`, `execute_payment`, `recover_inflight`, `release_payment_hold`, `requeue_payment`, and `reconcile_with_chain`, plus the five required invariants. I attempted the requested `--hypothesis-show-statistics` invocation, but this environment's pytest rejected that flag; the module itself still ran green under plain pytest, and the full suite also passed.
commands_run: pytest tests/test_payment_reconcile.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_social_route_tools.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --tb=no -q --no-header --hypothesis-show-statistics

pytest tests/test_payment_reconcile.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_social_route_tools.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --tb=no -q --no-header

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified `request_payment(...)` now delegates to `_normalize_inputs`, `_detect_collision_and_idempotent_row`, `_derive_amounts`, `_enforce_caps`, and `_persist_row`, with the public signature unchanged. The refactor survived both the targeted payment-module run and the full pytest suite, which is the regression gate for the byte-identical behavior claim.

Verified the verdict comment at the top of `tests/test_payment_race.py` cites `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` and Postgres read-committed isolation as the DB-level proof boundary, explicitly labels the test a logical-correctness sanity check, and does not contain the forbidden phrase `proven safe by this test`.

Verified the D11 reproduction uses distinct `source_channel_id` values and drives the downstream admin payment flow, not the intent-dedupe layer. The fix in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` uses `int(time.time() * 1000)` rather than a UUID suffix, and the regression test carries a verdict comment stating the collision was reproduced and fixed.

Verified `hypothesis` was added to `requirements.txt`, the `RuleBasedStateMachine` includes a `reconcile_with_chain` rule, and the file defines all five invariants. The terminal-state invariant only blocks backward transitions after `confirmed/cancelled`, so reconciliation from `failed/manual_hold` is intentionally not treated as a backward terminal transition.
